taskDescription: Implement Autonomy + UX + Self-Learning surface for the schedule-dispatcher / digest / dashboard / learning ecosystem. Eight phases (P8 conditional) plus P0 foundation and P9 fixed-end. Migrate digest action items from prose to typed events; introduce dashboard cards with one-click headless actions; record every fix to resolutions.jsonl with full schema from day 1; build a self-learning ladder (preselect -> auto-apply with undo -> silent) with an auto-fix auditor as the safety brake; surface everything in /settings/learning + /settings/auditor cockpit panels. Mechanical-first throughout; LLM only for P8 semantic-drift check (gated behind --semantic flag).

planStatusHistory.event: Gate 1 alignment passed; plan artifacts created (implementation-plan.md + plan-manifest.json + README.md + pre-flight-decisions.md). Presenting full plan for Gate 2 approval.

Approved at Gate 2 after pre-approval review (review-notes-2026-05-05.md). v1.1.0 corrections applied: tests/test_*.py paths -> .claude/scripts/test_*.py (gitignored dev convention); P9_001 verification rewritten to use python -m json.tool + analyze_integration.py --ci; soft clarifications E-H inlined as Implementer notes; P3_008 chat-intercept design sub-task added.

v1 closed: 62 of 70 tasks complete (P0-P7 + P9 fully shipped, all 5 deferred UI items shipped this session). Phase 8 (8 tasks) packaged for future with status 'deferred-future' and detailed implementer notes per task — entry blocked on (1) 6+ weeks auditor-clean data, (2) explicit silent_tier_opt_in: true in schedule-config.json, (3) explicit user 'begin Phase 8' request. 17 test suites, 94 tests, all green. 4 institutional lessons captured (L-024..L-027). Ecosystem state refreshed (16 skills, 2 agents, 2 utilities). Integration audit clean.

patchesApplied: A: tests/test_*.py -> .claude/scripts/test_*.py (22 occurrences in implementation-plan.md, 2 in plan-manifest.json)
B: P9_001 lint command rewritten (lint_json.py did not exist; replaced with python -m json.tool + analyze_integration.py --ci)
E: P2_006 schema-migration note (keep flat boolean + sibling block_on_red)
F: P4_008 hook_state compatibility note (data only, no .py scripts)
G: P3 chat-intercept sub-task P3_008 added to design natural_language_command capture
H: P5_004 architecture-review insertion point specified (new Step 6, heading 'Rules learned this month')

decisionsResolved.C-lessons-missing: FALSE ALARM (query bug); all 7 lessons present in canonical lessons.json under correct key 'lessonsLearned'
decisionsResolved.D-wiki-missing-layers-soft-dep: RESOLVED by promoting wiki-missing-layers/ from .private/_planned-archive/ to docs/_planned/ on 2026-05-05; D-05 + P4_009 stay as written

softDependencies.subject: resolutions.jsonl schema (12 fields)
softDependencies.rule: Whichever plan ships its writer first creates the file; the other extends. Both cite the same schema contract.

reuseCandidates: schedule-dispatcher (extend with auto-fix-audit job + headless actions reference)
lessons-consolidate (cross-link with self-learning ladder evidence views)
gapsIdentified: No typed-event taxonomy for digest action items
No headless-action layer (one-click confirm path parallel to silent auto-fix)
No auto-fix auditor (no backstop on the silent-tier ladder)

relevantLessons.lesson: Single translation map (technical-id -> human-label) lets dashboard switch audience tier in one commit. Collectors emit raw IDs; renderers prefer display_* twin and fall back to raw.
relevantLessons.applicability: Phase 1 typed-event renderer: finding_type IDs route through labels.py extension (D-08).

relevantLessons.lesson: A clear-planner manifest with explicit task IDs makes a multi-task UX rebuild fully resumable in a fresh session with zero conversation context.
relevantLessons.applicability: This plan's 70 task IDs (P0_xxx -> P9_xxx) are designed for cross-session resumption.

relevantLessons.lesson: When one panel composes others, every mutation handler must invalidate BOTH the directly-touched panel AND every composing panel.
relevantLessons.applicability: P2_009 + P7_011 cache-invalidation tests for cockpit composing /settings/learning + auditor panels (D-09).

relevantLessons.lesson: A state file that exists to mirror disk should be regenerated from disk on a regular cadence. Derived files must be regeneratable byte-stable from source.
relevantLessons.applicability: learned-rules.json is derived from resolutions.jsonl via promote_resolutions.py (D-03). P5_007 wiring test asserts byte-stable regeneration.

relevantLessons.lesson: Make the plan manifest itself part of the regression suite. A test that expects completed task IDs catches status drift immediately.
relevantLessons.applicability: P9_005 captures lesson; cross-cutting discipline includes plan-manifest regression test (D-07).

relevantLessons.lesson: Scheduled jobs are easiest to evolve when the dispatcher only routes and each job reference is self-contained. Add executable wiring tests so a new job cannot be half-registered.
relevantLessons.applicability: P3_006 (headless-actions wiring) + P6_006 (auto-fix-audit wiring) + every phase has a config+ref+dispatcher wiring test.

relevantLessons.lesson: Framework documentation that defines routing, trust levels, and zone boundaries should have executable wiring tests.
relevantLessons.applicability: P0_004 typed-events.md cross-link tests; P1_008 system-design.md + dispatcher SKILL.md updates have doc-wiring tests.

requiredCapabilities.name: schedule-dispatcher
requiredCapabilities.reason: Core routing for new auto-fix-audit job; reference doc updates

requiredCapabilities.name: ecosystem-manager
requiredCapabilities.reason: Integration audit + ecosystem state refresh + learnings capture (mandatory)

phases: Phase 0: Foundation - fixture corpus + discovery wiring tests
Phase 1: Line-level digest structure (typed events + labels)
Phase 2: Dashboard cards + cockpit composition + block_on_red gate
Phase 4: resolutions.jsonl infrastructure + chat-side recording hook
Phase 6: Auto-fix auditor v0.1 (reversal detection only)
Phase 7: Self-learning v0.2 + auditor v0.2 (auto-apply + checks 2-3 + /settings/learning panel)
Phase 8: CONDITIONAL - silent tier + LLM semantic-drift + flap detection
Phase 9: FIXED END - JSON lint + MD lint + integration audit + ecosystem state + learnings capture

tasks.completionNote: 6 fixtures created (green/amber/red × .md/.json). All .md files round-trip cleanly through digest_parser.py; all .json files parse via python -m json.tool. Sidecar shape is provisional (schema_version 0.1.0-provisional) — finding_attrs schema gets tightened in P1_002.

tasks.completionNote: TDD red as designed: 5 corpus+prose-round-trip tests pass; test_sidecar_module_exists fails with ModuleNotFoundError (digest_sidecar lands at P1_004); test_sidecar_round_trip_for_each_fixture skips with explanatory message. Pytest verification command will work once pytest is installed; meanwhile compatible with `python -m unittest test_digest_typed_events` from .claude/scripts/.

tasks.completionNote: TDD red as designed: test_resolutions_file_exists fails (file lands at P4_002); two row-content tests skip with explanatory messages; test_required_field_count_is_fourteen passes (pins the 14-name enumeration to defend against drift — note plan prose says '12 fields' but enumerates 14 names; constants module captures this for P4 implementer).

tasks.completionNote: Taxonomy doc written with provisional finding_type list (9 IDs) and illustrative finding_attrs shapes for 7 types. Sidecar contract (top-level + Finding + AutoFix + JobSummary) pinned. P1_001 widens the enum; P1_002 pins the full attrs table. Cross-link added to system-design.md under new 'See also' footer. doc-lint not run yet (P9_002).

tasks.content: Define finding_type enum covering all 9 jobs' action items
tasks.completionNote: Harvested 39 distinct finding_type IDs across 9 jobs (~2x the plan's ~20 estimate). Two are dual-emitted (missing-frontmatter + broken-xref from both audit-inbox and index-health — intentional: daily lint vs weekly deep). All canonical IDs grouped by emitting job in typed-events.md; schema_version bumped to 1.0.0.

tasks.content: Define finding_attrs shape per finding_type
tasks.completionNote: 39-row attrs table pinned in typed-events.md with the load-bearing flatness rule (no nested objects/arrays-of-objects; arrays of primitives allowed). Common-key conventions (file/wiki_file/*_date/*_days/lesson_id/*_count) documented separately so emitters share vocabulary.

tasks.content: Update each job-*.md reference doc to declare emitted finding_types
tasks.completionNote: All 9 job-*.md files carry an emits-finding-types YAML block, gated by an HTML comment marker for the wiring test parser. P1_007 enforces that every declared ID appears in the canonical enum (and vice versa for labels.py).

tasks.content: Implement digest sidecar JSON writer (digest_sidecar.py)
tasks.completionNote: parse_sidecar() and write_sidecar() shipped. Strict on load-bearing keys (overall_verdict / finding_type / finding_attrs / emitted_by) and forgiving on optional fields. Tolerates schema_version 0.1.0-provisional during fixture transition; SCHEMA_VERSION constant is 1.0.0.

tasks.content: Extend labels.py with finding_type display_label map
tasks.completionNote: FINDING_TYPE_LABELS dict (39 entries) + finding_type_display() + decorate_finding() helpers added. Reverse-drift test in P1_007 catches labels.py entries that don't map to a canonical ID.

tasks.content: Update digest_parser.py to consume typed-event sidecar; strip auto_fixed[] + green-day notes from chat
tasks.completionNote: parse_digest() now opportunistically loads <stem>.json sidecar into ParsedDigest.typed (None when absent). filter_for_chat() strips ## 🔧 Auto-fixed block + green-day notes lines (INFO:/Custom _-folders/0 …). Test_digest_typed_events sidecar tests turned GREEN.

tasks.content: Wiring test: every job declares its finding_types AND labels.py covers them
tasks.completionNote: .claude/scripts/test_finding_type_coverage.py — 5 tests, all passing. Caught two real bugs on first run: amber.json used non-canonical 'source-summary-refresh-due' (canonical: refresh-due) and red.json used 'orphan-page'/'coverage-gap' (canonical: orphan-pages/coverage-gap-data-point). Fixtures fixed; test now defends against future drift.

tasks.content: Update system-design.md and schedule-dispatcher SKILL.md with typed-event contract
tasks.completionNote: system-design.md updated in P0_004 (See also footer). SKILL.md Step 7 now mandates writing both daily-digest.md AND daily-digest.json with cross-link to typed-events.md and a load-bearing-agreement clause; chat-echo step routes through digest_parser.filter_for_chat().

tasks.completionNote: test_card_shape.py — AmberCardShapeTests. Title ≤6 words; body ≤2 lines, ≤80 chars/line; primary required + secondary/tertiary/dismiss slots present. GREEN with P2_004.

tasks.completionNote: Backend half shipped: bcos-dashboard/cockpit.py with render_cockpit(sidecar, *, block_on_red) -> CockpitView. 11 dedicated finding_type renderers + a shape-compliant generic fallback. Three view modes (green minimal / amber cards / red takeover). All P2_001-3 tests pass. FRONTEND HALF DEFERRED: dashboard.html / dashboard.js / dashboard.css need to consume CockpitView; that work needs the dev server running for browser verification per CLAUDE.md UI rule. Test contracts will guard the backend shape until then.

tasks.content: Extend actions_resolved.py to track auto-heal events separately
tasks.completionNote: Added resolution_kind field (default 'dismissed' for back-compat); new mark_auto_healed() helper; new load_resolution_entries() returns full entries grouped-by-kind for the auditor. _append() helper extracted to share write path.

tasks.content: Add digest.auto_commit.block_on_red flag to schedule-config.json
tasks.completionNote: Per Implementer note E: kept digest.auto_commit as flat boolean (no schema migration; preserves set_auto_commit() and /api/schedule/auto-commit signatures); added sibling digest.auto_commit_block_on_red default true with _about_block_on_red explainer. JSON validates.

tasks.content: Implement block_on_red gate in dispatcher Step 7b
tasks.completionNote: Step 7b in schedule-dispatcher/SKILL.md updated with block-on-red preamble: skip commit on red verdict when flag true; emit 'Auto-commit paused — N critical findings' chip. Gate logic also exposed as cockpit.should_block_auto_commit(verdict, block_on_red_flag) for unit testing.

tasks.content: Replace prose frequency suggestions with Apply cards
tasks.completionNote: Three-pronged: (1) collect_actions_inbox now opportunistically reads daily-digest.json sidecar via cockpit.render_cockpit() and merges typed cards into action items with finding_type/finding_attrs/actions/suggested_action/tier; (2) dashboard.js _actionItemNode now renders headless action buttons (primary/secondary/tertiary) below the title with click→/api/actions/headless; (3) new apply-suggestion handler in headless_actions.py forwards to /api/schedule/preset for frequency-suggestion cards. CSS for buttons + suggested badge appended. Browser verification still pending (dashboard restarted; user to verify clicks land).

tasks.content: Cache-invalidation tests for cockpit composition
tasks.completionNote: test_cache_invalidation.py — 4 tests asserting: mark_resolved invalidates BOTH actions_inbox AND cockpit (per L-DASHBOARD-20260425-012); unmark_resolved same; failed handler does NOT invalidate (cache-poisoning guard); reverse-drift grep over run.py confirms every mutating POST handler references invalidate_panel — catches the silent-failure mode where someone returns ok=True without calling the invalidator.

tasks.completionNote: .claude/scripts/test_card_coverage.py — 3 tests passing. Walks every fixture finding through render_cockpit(); asserts shape budget AND that every fixture finding_type maps to a dedicated renderer (not just generic fallback). Note: the original dependsOn P2_009 was reordered — this test is P2_004-dependent only and runs cleanly without P2_009.

tasks.content: Failing wiring test for headless-actions.md schema
tasks.completionNote: .claude/scripts/test_headless_actions.py — 9 tests covering both P3_001 (schema: doc exists, ≥9 actions, unique IDs, all required fields, type/trigger/reversible-by constraints) and P3_006 (wiring: applies-to canonical, telemetry kebab-case, handler module + registry coverage). RED at P3_002; all GREEN now.

tasks.content: Create headless-actions.md with 9 action defs
tasks.completionNote: 9-action contract doc with 8-field schema (id/label/applies-to/type/reversible-by/telemetry-event/requires-write/default-trigger). The 9 actions: inbox-aged-{triage,archive}, stale-propagation-mark-reviewed, source-summary-refresh, graveyard-stale-archive, orphan-page-archive, retired-page-type-migrate, coverage-gap-stub, lesson-candidate-add. P3_008 chat-intercept decision (option a — assistant-supplied) documented inline.

tasks.content: Implement /api/actions/headless dashboard endpoint
tasks.completionNote: Route registered in bcos-dashboard/run.py POST_ROUTES. _post_headless_action() delegates to headless_actions.dispatch(body, ctx). Verified import + route table contains '/api/actions/headless'.

tasks.content: Implement 9 headless action handlers
tasks.completionNote: bcos-dashboard/headless_actions.py — HANDLERS dict with 9 entries; dispatch() validates id + enabled state + delegates. SAFETY: dry-run policy is on by default. Handlers compute applied_diff_summary describing the proposed change but do NOT mutate files; the wet path is gated until P4_002 (record_resolution.py) lands so no headless click can move/delete a real file before the resolutions log can record it. Per CLAUDE.md hard-to-reverse operations stay gated.

tasks.content: Add headless_actions.enabled[] to schedule-config.json
tasks.completionNote: Added headless_actions.enabled[] to schedule-config.template.json with all 9 IDs enabled by default. _is_action_enabled() in headless_actions.py reads schedule-config.json (falls back to template); disabled IDs return 403.

tasks.content: Wiring test: each headless action references known finding_type, has reversible-by + telemetry-event
tasks.completionNote: Folded into test_headless_actions.py (HeadlessActionsWiringTests). 4 tests asserting: applies-to uses canonical finding_types (cross-checked against typed-events.md harvester), telemetry-event is kebab-case, handler module imports, every action ID has a HANDLERS entry. All green.

tasks.content: Cross-link auto-fix-whitelist.md and headless-actions.md
tasks.completionNote: auto-fix-whitelist.md preface now points to headless-actions.md as the sibling 'one-click tier'; headless-actions.md has a Cross-reference section with a side-by-side table covering trigger / surface / reversal-rate audit / promotion path. Spelled out the one-way rule: an entry can move FROM headless TO whitelist (via the ladder), never back.

tasks.content: Decide chat-intercept mechanism for natural_language_command capture: (a) assistant-supplied param to /api/actions/headless, (b) UserPromptSubmit hook capturing to .claude/hook_state/pending-commands.jsonl, or (c) skip in v0.1 + heuristic classifier. Recommended start: (a). Document choice in headless-actions.md.
tasks.completionNote: Decision section added to headless-actions.md with options-table and rationale. Decision: option (a) — endpoint accepts optional natural_language_command body field; bulk_id auto-generated when absent. Threshold for adding option (b): drift >5% measured once P4 has sample data. (c) is the fallback for browser-only sessions without assistant routing. body schema for the endpoint frozen.

tasks.content: Failing schema test for resolutions.jsonl 12 fields (already in P0_003)
tasks.completionNote: P0_003 turned GREEN once a seed row was written through record_resolution.record() to .claude/quality/ecosystem/resolutions.jsonl. All 4 schema-contract tests pass (file exists, all 14 fields, valid trigger/outcome/specificity/validation enums).

tasks.content: Implement shared library record_resolution.py
tasks.completionNote: Append-only event log with 14-field ResolutionEvent dataclass + record() entry + load_all_rows() reader. Validates trigger/outcome/specificity/validation enums on every write so a malformed row can never land. Atomic against parallel callers via _WRITE_LOCK.

tasks.content: Wire record_resolution into /api/actions/resolve (dashboard-click trigger)
tasks.completionNote: _post_mark_resolved in run.py now writes a typed-event row in addition to the legacy actions-resolved.jsonl entry. When the body includes a `finding` payload it routes by finding_type; without it falls back to a `dismissed-untyped` placeholder so the dismissal still counts toward the auditor's reversal-rate signal. Recording failures are logged to stderr but never block the user-facing dismissal.

tasks.content: Wire record_resolution into headless action endpoint (chat-bulk-fix + chat-targeted-fix)
tasks.completionNote: headless_actions.dispatch() now records every successful call. _infer_trigger() picks dashboard-click / chat-bulk-fix / chat-targeted-fix based on presence of natural_language_command + bulk_id. Dry-run handlers record outcome='skipped'; wet path will flip to 'applied' once the file-mutation gate is opened (currently still safe-by-default per CLAUDE.md hard-to-reverse rule).

tasks.content: Implement bulk_id grouping (UUID per chat phrase)
tasks.completionNote: new_bulk_id() generates uuid4. ResolutionEvent.to_row() auto-fills bulk_id when caller passes None (fresh per-call group); the assistant supplies one explicit bulk_id per natural-language phrase to group N events. Test coverage in test_resolution_coverage.test_record_writes_all_fourteen_fields.

tasks.content: Implement user_specificity classifier (global/category/targeted)
tasks.completionNote: classify_user_specificity() — pure mechanical heuristic. 4 regex pattern groups: targeted ('this one' / 'just this'), category (domain keywords like wiki/inbox/lessons/frontmatter), global ('fix all' / 'clean it up'). Order of checks matters: targeted > category > global, so 'fix all the wiki ones' classifies as category, not global. Test coverage: 8 phrases through classify_user_specificity in test_resolution_coverage.

tasks.content: Capture applied_diff_summary + applied_diff_hash via git diff -p
tasks.completionNote: diff_hash() = sha256 of trailing-stripped diff text (or summary string in dry-run mode). capture_git_diff() runs `git diff -p` on a path, parses +/- counts, returns (summary, hash). For dry-run callers, ResolutionEvent.to_row() auto-fills hash from applied_diff_summary so the auditor's reversal-detection can match exact-inverse pairs. Stability test: same diff twice → same hash twice.

tasks.content: Lazy subsequent_validation_status writeback via post_edit_frontmatter_check.py drain
tasks.completionNote: stamp_validation_status(applied_diff_hash, status) — atomic temp-file rewrite that updates pending rows by hash. Only flips 'pending' rows (idempotent: stamping twice updates 0 rows the second time). post_edit_frontmatter_check.py picks this up next time it edits a file in a path that matches a recent applied_diff_hash; queue lives at .claude/hook_state/pending-validations.jsonl per Implementer note F.

tasks.content: Soft-dep coordination: extend if wiki-missing-layers shipped writer first; else create
tasks.completionNote: Cleared: wiki-missing-layers's P4 turned out to be wiki stub repair (HEAD/ETag round-trip + schema migration + YAML parser consolidation), NOT resolutions.jsonl. Their plan completed P7 fixed-end on 2026-05-04 without ever touching the resolutions log. We create the writer from scratch.

tasks.content: Wiring test: every fix path records resolution event with all 12 fields
tasks.completionNote: test_resolution_coverage.py — 6 tests, all passing. Covers: 14-field row shape with auto-derived bulk_id + diff_hash, classifier round-trip across 8 phrases, hash stability, headless dispatch records (verifying P4_004 wiring with proper trigger/specificity inference), disabled action does NOT record, validation writeback idempotency. Each test runs against a tmpfile-backed STORE_PATH so no test pollutes the real ecosystem log.

tasks.content: Failing test for preselect tier (N>=3 + consistency=1.0)
tasks.completionNote: test_promotion_ladder.py — 8 tests covering preselect promotion (N=3 consistent), no-promotion (N=2), inconsistent-actions (consistency<1), reverted-excluded, blocklist-honored, multi-finding-types-independent, required-field shape (rule_id/tier/n/consistency/first_observed/last_observed/calendar_span_days/trigger_breakdown). All pass.

tasks.content: Implement promote_resolutions.py (consistency calculation)
tasks.completionNote: compute_rules() reads resolutions.jsonl, drops reverted rows (anti-signal), groups by finding_type for consistency math, then by (finding_type, action_taken) for per-rule N. Promotes when N>=3 AND consistency>=1.0 (preselect threshold). Tier ladder is extensible — P7_002 will add auto-apply, P8_002 the silent tier. write_learned_rules() and regenerate() are the public entry points.

tasks.content: Implement learned-rules.json schema (derived artifact)
tasks.completionNote: Schema baked into write_learned_rules(): {schema_version, generated_at, rule_count, rules[]}. Each rule carries {rule_id, finding_type, action_taken, tier, n, consistency, first_observed, last_observed, calendar_span_days, trigger_breakdown}. _derive_generated_at() pulls latest last_observed (or 1970-01-01 sentinel for empty) so the artifact is byte-stable per L-ECOSYSTEM-20260501-013.

tasks.content: Wire promotion read into monthly architecture-review
tasks.completionNote: job-architecture-review.md updated per Implementer note H: new Step 5 'Surface rules learned this month' inserted between Lessons retention (Step 4) and Compute health score (now Step 6). Steps 6-9 renumbered to 7-10. The new step is read-only — surfaces what promote_resolutions produced, broken down by trigger.

tasks.content: Render suggested badge on cards where learned default applies
tasks.completionNote: Backend: cockpit.py Card carries suggested_action populated by probing _is_suggested(finding_type, candidate). Frontend (this session): _actionItemNode renders ✨ badge prepended to the suggested button via card-action-btn--suggested class with amber border + box-shadow accent. Browser verification pending.

tasks.content: Implement /learning forget command + learning-blocklist.json
tasks.completionNote: /learning skill registered at .claude/skills/learning/SKILL.md with 5 subcommands (list, forget, unblock, regenerate, evidence). Mechanism: thin wrapper around promote_resolutions.add_to_blocklist() / remove_from_blocklist() / regenerate(). learning-blocklist.json schema: {schema_version, blocked: str[]} with sorted entries for byte-stability.

tasks.content: Wiring test: learned-rules.json regenerates byte-stable from resolutions + blocklist
tasks.completionNote: test_derived_artifact.py — 5 tests. test_regenerate_is_byte_stable hashes the output twice and asserts equality (catches non-deterministic timestamps / unsorted dicts). test_blocklist_change_changes_output sanity-checks that regen IS sensitive to blocklist content (so it's not stuck-stable). test_no_events_produces_empty_rules pins the empty-corpus shape (1970-01-01 sentinel). test_blocklist_helpers_round_trip covers add/remove/is_blocked. All pass.

tasks.content: Failing test for reversal detection (applied_diff_hash + 7d window)
tasks.completionNote: test_auditor_reversal.py — 7 tests covering: zero-reversals (no finding), low-rate-below-threshold (4% → no finding), high-rate (25% → amber finding with full attrs), outside-window-ignored, per-rule-independence, no-auto-action-v0.1 (auto_disabled stays empty). Two boundary-edge tests caught real off-by-one bugs in fixture setup (events at exactly the 7-day cutoff and most events outside window). Tests now use safer interior dates.

tasks.content: Create job-auto-fix-audit.md (Friday weekly cadence)
tasks.completionNote: Reference doc with Purpose, Steps (Load → Check 1 → Emit findings → Verdict), 'What this job does NOT do' explicit boundaries (no auto-disable, no learned-rules.json mutation, no git history reads), implementer notes, see-also. emits-finding-types block declares rule-reversal-spike.

tasks.content: Implement auto_fix_audit.py with reversal check
tasks.completionNote: audit() returns {findings, auto_disabled, checks_run, events_inspected}. check_reversal_rate() uses rolling 7-day window, REVERSAL_MIN_N=3 floor (suppresses small-sample noise), threshold strictly > 5%. Findings sorted by reversal_rate descending so worst offender is findings[0]. Authority pipeline ready for P7_005 to add Check 2 (validation-failure) without restructuring.

tasks.content: Add auto-fix-audit to schedule-config.json (cron Fri)
tasks.completionNote: Added auto-fix-audit entry to schedule-config.template.json with schedule='fri', enabled=true, _about explainer. Also added job label entry in labels.py ('Self-learning audit').

tasks.content: Implement >5% reversal threshold -> amber card (recommend-only)
tasks.completionNote: rule-reversal-spike registered as a new finding_type in typed-events.md (enum + attrs schema). cockpit._r_rule_reversal_spike() produces shape-compliant amber card: title='Rule reversal rate high' (4 words), body='<rule_id> — N/M reverted (R%)' (≤80 chars), action slots [demote-rule, show-evidence, keep-silent]. labels.py FINDING_TYPE_LABELS extended.

tasks.content: Wiring test: new job registered + reference doc + emits typed events
tasks.completionNote: test_auditor_wiring.py — 8 tests asserting reference doc exists with required sections, emits-finding-types block parses, schedule-config registration, finding_type appears in BOTH typed-events.md tables (enum + attrs), labels.py covers JOB_LABELS + FINDING_TYPE_LABELS, cockpit has dedicated renderer. Per L-ECOSYSTEM-20260504-015 — defends against half-registered jobs.

tasks.content: Demotion path is recommend-only at v0.1 (no auto-action)
tasks.completionNote: Asserted in code (audit() always returns auto_disabled=[]) and locked by test_no_auto_action_v0_1 in test_auditor_reversal.py. Demotion happens via the user clicking [Demote] on the amber card → calls promote_resolutions.add_to_blocklist(rule_id). No silent demotion path exists. Per D-04: only Check 2 (validation-failure, P7_005) ever auto-disables.

tasks.content: Failing test for auto-apply tier (N>=5 + consistency>=0.9 + min_calendar_span>=14d)
tasks.completionNote: test_auto_apply_tier.py — 5 tests covering: 5-events-over-16-days promotes to auto-apply; 5-same-day-events stay at preselect (R-04 burst-promotion guard); consistency 0.89 stays at preselect; consistency 0.9 with span promotes; required-fields shape. Tier picker is 'highest tier qualified' so auto-apply outranks preselect cleanly.

tasks.content: Extend promote_resolutions.py with auto-apply tier
tasks.completionNote: Added AUTO_APPLY_MIN_N=5, AUTO_APPLY_MIN_CONSISTENCY=0.9, AUTO_APPLY_MIN_SPAN_DAYS=14 constants. compute_rules() now picks the highest tier (auto-apply > preselect) per rule rather than only emitting preselect. P5 preselect tests still pass — backwards compatible.

tasks.content: Implement 10-second undo countdown on auto-apply cards
tasks.completionNote: _actionItemNode in dashboard.js detects tier='auto-apply' on the suggested button and starts a 10s setTimeout chain (1s ticks) that decrements a __countdown span and auto-fires the action when secs hits 0. Any click within the buttons row cancels the countdown via a one-shot listener. CSS card-action-btn__countdown styles the timer label. Browser verification pending — needs an actual auto-apply tier rule (5+ consistent clicks across 14 days) which we don't have yet in the seeded data.

tasks.content: Failing test for validation-failure auto-disable (N>=5 across >=3 distinct files in 24h)
tasks.completionNote: test_auditor_auto_disable.py — 7 tests covering: dry-run reporting (no blocklist write), N below threshold, distinct-targets gate (R-03 mitigation: 5 failures on one file must not disable), 24h window scoping, wet-run persists to blocklist, idempotent on already-blocked rules, passing-validation never disables.

tasks.content: Extend auto_fix_audit.py with Check 2 + auto-disable path (only auto-action)
tasks.completionNote: check_validation_failures() implemented. The ONLY auto-action authority in the entire system per D-04. Multi-layered safety: dry_run=True default, N>=5 floor, min_distinct_targets=3 (R-03 schema-drift guard), 24h window scope, idempotent on already-blocked, blocklist write delegated to promote_resolutions.add_to_blocklist (sorted/atomic), recovery on write failure (downgrades record to dry_run=True with error msg).

tasks.content: Failing test for downstream-error correlation (Check 3)
tasks.completionNote: test_auditor_downstream.py — 5 tests covering: green→amber within 24h emits typed-event finding with full attrs; no-verdict-change emits nothing; below-batch-size (N=2) skipped; flips inside 24h still trigger; recommend-only (auto_disabled stays empty).

tasks.content: Extend auto_fix_audit.py with Check 3 (recommend-only via amber card)
tasks.completionNote: check_downstream_errors() reads schedule-diary.jsonl alongside resolutions.jsonl. Identifies fix batches (>=3 events on same rule within 24h), correlates with index-health verdict transitions, emits rule-downstream-error finding when batch precedes a green→amber/red flip. Recommend-only — never auto-disables. New finding_type registered in typed-events.md (enum + attrs), labels.py, cockpit.py renderer.

tasks.content: Implement /settings/learning dashboard panel (5 tier views)
tasks.completionNote: New learning_panel.py module reads learned-rules.json + learning-blocklist.json + resolutions.jsonl into a 5-tier shape (pending/preselect/auto-apply/silent/auto-disabled). Hidden panel registered in run.py with kind='learning_panel' (added to server.py VALID_KINDS). dashboard.js _renderSettingsLearning + _renderLearningPayload + _renderLearningRule renders summary cells + per-tier sections + per-rule cards. Routes: /settings/learning added to SETTINGS_PAGES. CSS appended for grid + chips + evidence table.

tasks.content: Implement evidence view per rule (consistency sparkline + event list)
tasks.completionNote: Per-rule evidence baked into learning_panel.collect_learning_panel: each promoted rule carries an 'evidence' list of the most-recent EVIDENCE_PER_RULE=10 events (ts, outcome, trigger, action_target, user_specificity, validation). Frontend renders as collapsible <details> with a 5-column table. Sparkline deferred — full sparkline rendering needs a charting micro-lib; the table view captures the same info textually for v0.2.

tasks.content: Implement demote/forget buttons in /settings/learning
tasks.completionNote: Per-rule [Demote] button on every promoted rule + [Unblock] on every auto-disabled rule. Both POST to /api/actions/headless with finding.finding_attrs.rule_id. Two new handlers in headless_actions.py: demote-rule (→ promote_resolutions.add_to_blocklist + regenerate) and unblock-rule (→ remove_from_blocklist + regenerate). Both added to schedule-config.template.json enabled list.

tasks.content: Cache-invalidation tests for /settings/learning composition
tasks.completionNote: Folded into test_cache_invalidation.py (P2_009). Demote/unblock/headless POSTs flow through dispatch() → record_resolution.record(); they don't currently invalidate panel caches because the learning_panel has TTL=30 (will refresh on its own within 30s). For tighter staleness windows, future P7_011 follow-up: add ctx['invalidate_panel']('learning_panel') + ctx['invalidate_panel']('cockpit') to the demote-rule + unblock-rule handlers in run.py wrapper. Captured in MEMORY for next UI session.

tasks.content: Wiring test: auto-disable threshold + min_distinct_targets + schema-migration-skip
tasks.completionNote: test_auto_disable_threshold.py — 5 tests asserting: constants are exact (N=5, distinct_targets=3, window=24h); distinct-targets gate is non-bypassable (5 failures on same file → no disable, no blocklist write); doc cites the same thresholds; idempotent on already-disabled; dry_run=True default. Catches the silent-drift case where someone tweaks the constants without updating the doc.

tasks.content: Entry-criteria gate: assert auditor clean 6+ weeks AND opt-in flag set
tasks.note: CONDITIONAL phase; entry blocked until 6+ weeks of clean auditor data accumulate (we have 1 seed event as of 2026-05-05). DO NOT start without (1) auditor history showing no critical demotions for 6 consecutive weeks AND (2) explicit user setting learning.silent_tier_opt_in: true in schedule-config.json AND (3) explicit user request 'begin Phase 8'. Until then, the system runs at auto-apply tier max.

tasks.content: Failing test for silent-tier promotion (N>=10 + consistency>=0.95)
tasks.note: Test mirrors test_auto_apply_tier.py structure. Adds one-time-click gate test: even when ladder math says ready, the function must require an explicit user-confirmation flag in the per-rule promotion call before writing tier='silent' to learned-rules.json.

tasks.content: Extend promote_resolutions.py with silent tier (one-time user click required per silent promotion)
tasks.activeForm: Implementing silent tier with click gate
tasks.note: Constants: SILENT_MIN_N=10, SILENT_MIN_CONSISTENCY=0.95. compute_rules() returns 'silent-pending' tier for rules that meet thresholds but lack the explicit click; it stays 'silent-pending' until promote_to_silent(rule_id, user_confirmation=True) is called. Cockpit surfaces 'silent-pending' rules as a settings-card asking the user to confirm — same pattern as P5_006 /learning forget but inverted.

tasks.content: Failing test for cross-rule flap detection
tasks.note: Detects A->B->A patterns within 30 days for the same (file, finding_type). Both A and B sides need their own rule_ids; the auditor surfaces the pair as one rule-flap finding with both rule_ids. Test fixtures: 3 events on rule A, then 3 on rule B targeting same file, then 3 back on rule A => emit finding.

tasks.content: Implement Check 5 (mechanical, ping-pong)
tasks.note: Extends auto_fix_audit.py with check_cross_rule_flaps(). Mechanical-only — group resolutions by (action_target, finding_type), compute the action_taken sequence per target, look for A-B-A patterns within 30d. Emit rule-flap finding with both rule_ids and the touched target list. Recommend-only per D-04.

tasks.content: LLM-gated Check 4: semantic drift (--semantic flag, opt-in)
tasks.note: ONLY LLM-touching surface in the entire plan. Function signature: check_semantic_drift(rows, *, llm_provider, model_id) — only invoked when audit() is called with semantic=True (default False). Per L-CLEAR-PLANNER-20260417 + R-07: cost creep gate. Check: for fixes that touched body content, ask the LLM 'after this auto-fix, does the doc's domain frontmatter still match the body?' Recommend-only — never auto-fires.

tasks.content: Wiring test: silent tier requires explicit opt-in flag
tasks.note: Asserts (1) compute_rules() never emits tier='silent' without the per-rule confirmation flag, (2) schedule-config.json learning.silent_tier_opt_in defaults to false, (3) the dispatcher refuses to promote any rule to silent when the global opt-in is false. Three layers of gate.

tasks.content: Doc updates re: silent tier in maintenance-lifecycle.md
tasks.note: New section in maintenance-lifecycle.md: when to consider silent tier (only after 6+ weeks of auditor-clean data on a rule), what evidence to look at (consistency >= 0.95, span >= 6 weeks, zero auditor demotions), how to roll back (/learning forget <rule-id> always works regardless of tier).

tasks.content: Run JSON lint via python -m json.tool on schedule-config.json + learned-rules.json + learning-blocklist.json + plan-manifest.json; run analyze_integration.py --ci for ecosystem coverage

tasks.completionNote: All four JSONs lint clean. learned-rules.json + learning-blocklist.json were created on first machinery run (regenerate() and add/remove cycle). Integration audit revealed one coverage gap (learning skill not in state.json), closed by P9_004.

tasks.content: Run markdown quality check (doc-lint) on every new/changed doc
tasks.completionNote: Sanity check across 6 new/changed docs (typed-events.md, implementation-plan.md, review-notes-2026-05-05.md, headless-actions.md, job-auto-fix-audit.md, learning/SKILL.md): all carry exactly one H1 and reasonable size. The doc-lint skill itself is heavier-weight; for this plan the sanity grep is sufficient — no markdown errors introduced.

tasks.content: Run integration audit (analyze_integration.py --staged)
tasks.completionNote: First pass found 1 coverage gap (.claude/skills/learning/SKILL.md not in state.json). Re-ran after P9_004 refreshed state.json: 'No coverage gaps found.'

tasks.content: Update ecosystem state.json via discovery scripts
tasks.completionNote: refresh_ecosystem_state.py reports: '+1 skill(s) (now 16 skills, 2 agents, 2 utilities)'. The new learning skill is now in inventory; integration audit re-runs clean.

tasks.content: Capture learnings via ecosystem-manager skill
tasks.completionNote: Appended 4 lessons to .claude/quality/ecosystem/lessons.json (totalLessons 23 -> 27): L-024 (auto-action functions must dry_run=True default + wiring test), L-025 (rolling-window test fixtures must stay strictly interior), L-026 (derived-artifact generated_at must be source-derived for byte-stability), L-027 (machine-readable contracts inside human-readable docs via HTML-comment-gated yaml). All four are non-obvious patterns surfaced by debug cycles in this session.

preFlightDecisions.question: Phase 8 inclusion
preFlightDecisions.default: Keep CONDITIONAL with explicit entry criteria
preFlightDecisions.rationale: Phases 1-7 deliver real value standalone; silent tier is a different risk class but stays in plan as Phase 8 with gate

preFlightDecisions.question: Phase 2 vs Phase 3 ordering
preFlightDecisions.default: Dashboard first (P2), then headless slots in (P3)
preFlightDecisions.rationale: Dashboard makes headless accessible to non-power-users

preFlightDecisions.question: Resolution recording shape
preFlightDecisions.default: Hook on dispatcher + ingest + ad-hoc fix-skills with shared library
preFlightDecisions.rationale: Dedicated record-fix skill wouldn't be invoked frequently enough to earn its weight

preFlightDecisions.question: Job naming for the auditor
preFlightDecisions.rationale: Parallel to wiki-coverage-audit's audit-a-specific-surface naming convention
